title: Linear-in-Means Estimation with 2SLS and ARD
text: vignette, demonstrate perform linear--means estimation peer effects network adjacency matrix GG unknown. Instead GG, observe Aggregated Relational Data (ARD), use estimate network’s spectral structure. use estimate construct instruments two-stage least squares (2SLS) estimation using ivreg package.

title: Linear-in-Means Estimation with 2SLS and ARD
text: Consider standard linear--means model outcome yy: y=αι+βGy+γW+δGW+ηX+ϵy = \alpha \iota + \beta G y + \gamma W + \delta G W + \eta X + \epsilon : - GG row-normalized adjacency matrix. - WW vector node-level exogenous covariates. - XX latent positions underlying network model. - GyGy represents endogenous peer effect. - GWGW represents contextual effects.

title: Linear-in-Means Estimation with 2SLS and ARD
text: First, simulate network ARD using package’s built-simulation tools. use n=1000n = 1000 nodes k=2k = 2 latent dimensions.
code: set.seed(42) n <- 1000 k <- 2  # Simulate ARD data sim <- simulate_ard_data(   n = n, k = k, corr = 0.8,   num_good_traits = 5, num_bad_traits = 0 )  A <- sim$A traits <- sim$traits ard <- sim$ard X <- sim$s_pop$u %*% diag(sqrt(sim$s_pop$d)) # True latent positions  # True parameters for linear-in-means model alpha <- 1 beta <- 0.5 gamma <- 2 delta <- 1 eta <- c(0.5, -0.5) # Coefficients for latent positions  # Generate exogenous covariate W W <- rnorm(n)  # Row-normalize A to get G row_sums <- rowSums(A) row_sums[row_sums == 0] <- 1 G <- A / row_sums  # Solve for endogenous y:  # y = (I - beta * G)^{-1} * (alpha + gamma * W + delta * G * W + X * eta + eps) eps <- rnorm(n, sd = 0.5) I <- Diagonal(n) rhs <- alpha + gamma * W + delta * (G %*% W) + (as.matrix(X) %*% eta) + eps y <- as.numeric(solve(I - beta * G, rhs))

title: Linear-in-Means Estimation with 2SLS and ARD
text: Next, estimate network spectral structure ARD. assume node degrees (row sums AA) known use normalize spectral estimate.
code: estimate <- estimate_spectrum(ard, traits)  # Extract estimated components Utilde <- estimate$Utilde Sigmatilde <- estimate$Sigmatilde  # Construct P_tilde = Utilde %*% Sigmatilde %*% t(Utilde) # This serves as a plug-in for the unknown adjacency matrix A P_tilde <- tcrossprod(Utilde %*% Sigmatilde, Utilde)  # Normalize P_tilde using KNOWN degrees to get G_hat G_hat <- P_tilde / row_sums

title: Linear-in-Means Estimation with 2SLS and ARD
text: use Ĝ2W\hat{G}^2 W ĜX\hat{G} X instruments endogenous peer effect GyGy. also include WW, GWGW, latent positions XX covariates.
code: # Prepare peer effects and instruments Gy <- as.numeric(G %*% y) Gw <- as.numeric(G %*% W) G2w_hat <- as.numeric(G_hat %*% (G_hat %*% W))  # GX latent positions as instruments Gx_hat <- as.matrix(G_hat %*% X) colnames(Gx_hat) <- c("Gx1", "Gx2")  # Latent positions as covariates X1 <- X[, 1] X2 <- X[, 2]  # Perform 2SLS using ivreg # Model: y ~ Gy + W + Gw + X1 + X2 # Instruments for Gy: G2w_hat + Gx1 + Gx2 model_2sls <- ivreg(   y ~ Gy + W + Gw + X1 + X2 |      G2w_hat + Gx_hat + W + Gw + X1 + X2 )  summary(model_2sls) #>  #> Call: #> ivreg(formula = y ~ Gy + W + Gw + X1 + X2 | G2w_hat + Gx_hat +  #>     W + Gw + X1 + X2) #>  #> Residuals: #>      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  #> -1.87373 -0.33386  0.01529  0.33654  1.62678  #>  #> Coefficients: #>             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)     #> (Intercept)  0.60807    0.31066   1.957 0.050586 .   #> Gy           0.79920    0.21050   3.797 0.000156 *** #> W            1.97456    0.01660 118.961  < 2e-16 *** #> Gw           0.36282    0.45950   0.790 0.429954     #> X1           0.63789    0.08537   7.472 1.73e-13 *** #> X2          -0.39107    0.09033  -4.329 1.65e-05 *** #>  #> Diagnostic tests: #>                  df1 df2 statistic p-value     #> Weak instruments   3 992    725.40  <2e-16 *** #> Wu-Hausman         1 993      0.04   0.842     #> Sargan             2  NA      0.35   0.839     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Residual standard error: 0.502 on 994 degrees of freedom #> Multiple R-Squared: 0.9398,  Adjusted R-squared: 0.9395  #> Wald test:  3102 on 5 and 994 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

title: Linear-in-Means Estimation with 2SLS and ARD
text: substituting spectral estimate Ĝ\hat{G} unknown row-normalized adjacency matrix GG, leveraging known degrees, can successfully recover peer effect estimates. Including latent positions estimated neighborhood averages instruments significantly aids identification.

title: Spectral Estimation of Low-Rank Models from Aggregated Relational Data (ARD)
text: Efficient Spectral Estimation: Estimate latent positions community structures directly ARD using estimate_spectrum(). Sparse Graph Sampling: Generate representative graphs estimated models using sample_graph(), support large networks memory-efficient chunked sampling. Model-Based Simulation: Generate synthetic ARD datasets using simulate_ard_data(). function generates network Degree-Corrected Stochastic Block Model (DCSBM) simulates traits correlated underlying latent positions. ARD formed aggregating traits neighborhood node.

title: Spectral Estimation of Low-Rank Models from Aggregated Relational Data (ARD)
text: example demonstrates simulate ARD data, estimate underlying network model, analyze results. estimation returns spectral decomposition underlying network model. can use estimated latent positions Utilde recover community structure (e.g., via k-means clustering) sample new graphs. Adjusted Rand Index (ARI) 1.0 indicates clustering perfectly recovers true community structure noisy ARD. Even 0.80.8 correlation traits latent positions, presence uninformative traits, spectral estimation accurately identifies underlying blocks.
